reasoning: Query asks specifically for cherry blossom (sakura) spots in Tohoku, but the tool returned general Akita spots—castles, Lake Tazawa, volcano, fireworks—with no sakura_meisho_100 kind surfaced. The Omagari fireworks (summer) is actively misleading for a sakura searcher. Only one Tohoku prefecture covered.
missing_data: Sakura-specific spots: Kakunodate weeping cherries (sakura_meisho_100), Hirosaki Park, Kitakami Tenshochi, Hanamiyama; other Tohoku prefectures (Aomori, Iwate, Miyagi, Fukushima, Yamagata) not queried.
wrong_data: No factual errors but fireworks festival entirely off-topic for the cherry blossom constraint.
improvement_hint: Filter or boost sakura_meisho_100 kind when query intent is sakura; fan out to all six Tohoku prefectures instead of only Akita.

reasoning: Returns 24 Niigata food/sake items including top brands (Koshi no Kanbai, Hakkaisan, Kubota) plus GI products. METI-authority data is accurate and well-described. Minor gap: GI items like kurosaki chamame (edamame) dilute focus—agent must filter. No venue addresses for brewery tours.
missing_data: Physical brewery addresses/visitor info for sake tourism; Kubota brewery (Asahi Shuzo, Nagaoka) location not given. Sake tourism experiences missing.
improvement_hint: Add a sake_brewery kind tag to wikidata venue cross-references so the agent can surface actual visit-able brewery locations alongside the brand records.

reasoning: Query asks for ski resorts WITH onsen in Hakuba. Response returns Happo-One ski resort (great) plus a lake, art museum, botanical garden—none with onsen. No onsen ryokan or spa data returned. Constraint (ski+onsen combo) only half-satisfied.
missing_data: Hakuba onsen ryokans (Happo Onsen, Mimizuku no Yado etc.); no hotel/onsen data for Hakuba; Cortina, Goryu ski areas with onsen missing.
improvement_hint: Cross-call get_hotels with onsen filter for Hakuba municipality alongside get_spots; add onsen_tag field to spots so ski+onsen pairing is explicit.

reasoning: Query asks for Kansai autumn foliage spots but tool returns generic Kyoto spots (Nijo Castle, To-ji, Nagaoka-kyo ancient capital) with no koyo/momiji-specific kinds. Only Kyoto queried; Osaka, Nara, Hyogo (Kansai) excluded. Spots returned are famous but not specifically foliage-selected.
missing_data: Koyo-specific sites: Eikan-do, Tofuku-ji, Arashiyama for Kyoto; Minoo Park (Osaka); Yoshino for Nara; season filter not applied. Kansai fan-out missing.
improvement_hint: Detect koyo/autumn-foliage intent and boost spots with momiji/koyo kinds; fan out across all Kansai prefectures when region-level query is detected.

reasoning: Returns Yoshino (top-ranked, sakura_meisho_100 with prominence 1.1), Kofuku-ji, Koriyama Castle—Yoshino is Japan's most famous cherry spot and directly answers the query. Coverage is Nara only; Osaka and Hyogo sakura sites absent. Solid for the tool scope though.
missing_data: Other Kansai sakura sites: Maruyama Park (Kyoto), Osaka Castle moat cherries, Himeji Castle. Single prefecture limits coverage.
improvement_hint: Fan out to Kyoto and Osaka when sakura intent + Kansai region detected; surface sakura_meisho_100 kind prominently in ranking.

reasoning: Query asks for stargazing on Okinawa's remote islands. Response returns mainland Okinawa castles (Nakagusuku, Zakimi) and Naha monuments—none are remote islands, none are dark-sky sites. Kumejima appears (nagisa_100) but not for stargazing. Constraint (離島 + 星空) entirely unaddressed.
missing_data: Miyako-jima, Iriomote, Taketomi, Ishigaki dark-sky sites; star_park or dark_sky kind not surfaced; Kumejima Star Park specifically famous for stargazing.
improvement_hint: Add dark_sky/star_park kind tags to known stargazing sites on remote Okinawan islands; detect 星空/stargazing intent and filter by island municipality codes.

reasoning: Returns onsen ryokan (hito_yu_kai members including Shiranui Onsen, Ginkonyu, Daisetsuzan area) which fits the rural onsen intent. However urban hotels (ANA Holiday Inn Sapporo Susukino) mixed in—city hotel doesn't match rural countryside constraint. Coordinates null on ryokan records limits navigability.
missing_data: Coordinates for rural ryokan listings; Noboribetsu, Jozankei, Sounkyo onsen town context; English descriptions absent for hito_yu_kai entries.
improvement_hint: Filter or deprioritize urban hotels when rural (田舎) intent is detected; populate coordinates for hito_yu_kai entries to enable map display.

reasoning: Query is about Seto Inland Sea islands (Shodoshima, Naoshima, Teshima etc.) but response returns general Kagawa spots: Ritsurin Garden, Mannō Lake, Marugame Castle—all on Kagawa mainland. Island-specific content entirely absent. Core intent (島々) not addressed.
missing_data: Naoshima (art island), Shodoshima (olive/soy sauce island), Teshima, Ōshima; Hiroshima/Okayama Setouchi islands; island kind filter missing.

reasoning: Query asks for indigo dyeing workshops but response returns METI craft designations (Awa Hon-ai Shijira Weave, Otani Ware pottery, Awa Washi paper)—correct official crafts but no workshop/experience venue data. User wanting hands-on dyeing workshops gets product descriptions, not actionable venues.

reasoning: Excellent match. Query asks for Koyasan shukubo temple lodging and returns confirmed shukubo association members (Annyoin, Ichijoin, Ekoin, Kitamuroin) with rich Japanese descriptions of experience, cuisine, and history. Authority = Koyasan Shukubo Association. Highly actionable, phone numbers provided.
missing_data: English names and descriptions for shukubo entries (name_en is null); coordinates absent; website URLs sparse—limits agent's ability to present to non-Japanese speakers.
improvement_hint: Add English translations for shukubo descriptions and populate website URLs/coordinates from Koyasan Shukubo Association website to improve English-language usability.

reasoning: 153 results including Kakunodate samurai district (Akita, prominent with rich description), Matsue Buke Yashiki (Shimane), Fukui Uchiyama residence, and Tokyo gate—good national spread. Kakunodate entry has sakura and preservation district tags. Query asks for 'outside Kyoto' which is satisfied by multi-prefecture spread.
missing_data: Chiran samurai district (Kagoshima), Kitsuki (Oita), Tsuwano (Shimane); some entries lack English descriptions or coordinates.
improvement_hint: Boost entries with preservation_district + buke_yashiki combination and ensure top results are named districts rather than individual gates; add Kagoshima and Oita entries.

reasoning: Query asks for summer festivals (夏祭り) in Akita but returns only 3 items—two are intangible folk arts (Bugaku, Kakunodate Festival) without summer dates, one has a 1705 date. Akita's world-famous summer festivals (Kanto, Nebuta in Aomori, Sansa Odori) entirely absent. Only 3 results for a prefecture famous for summer festivals is a severe coverage failure.
missing_data: Akita Kanto Matsuri (most famous Akita summer festival, August), Nishimonai Bon Odori, Omagari Fireworks (August)—all missing from festival results.
wrong_data: Hayachine Kagura in national_heritage is an Iwate festival, not Akita—cross-prefecture bleed.
improvement_hint: Ingest scraped_schema_event data for Akita prefecture; Kanto Matsuri should be in corpus; add hanabi/summer_festival kind filter for seasonal intent detection.

reasoning: Query asks for small fishing port towns (漁港の町) in San'in region. Response returns Lake Shinji, Matsue Castle, Iwami Ginzan Silver Mine, Mt. Sanbe—all famous Shimane sites but none are fishing port towns. Core intent entirely missed; coastal fishing village data absent.
missing_data: Mitsu fishing port, Hamada port town, Ube/Masuda coastal areas; Tottori coast (Uradome) fishing communities; fishing_port kind tag; Kotoura/Misasa coastal towns.
improvement_hint: Add fishing_port/gyoko kind to coastal municipalities; detect 漁港/port intent and filter by coastal location using prefecture coastline data.

reasoning: Query asks where to experience washi papermaking in Gifu. Returns 8 craft items including Mino Washi (meti_densan:0910 expected but not visible in truncated view)—however top results are Mino Ware pottery, Hida Shunkei lacquer, lathe work before washi. missing_data: Mino Washi Akari Museum (workshop venue), Warabi paper studio experiences in Mino City; specific studio addresses; washi ranked below pottery in results.

reasoning: Query asks for fishing villages on the Noto Peninsula. Response returns Kanazawa (Kanazawa Castle, Kenroku-en, Oyama Shrine)—all city attractions in central Ishikawa, not Noto Peninsula coastal villages. Noto municipality filtering missing entirely.
missing_data: Noto Peninsula fishing villages: Wajima (lacquerware/morning market), Suzu, Notojima, Noto-cho coastal communities; wajima_morningmarket kind; Noto coastal road towns.

reasoning: 91 results for 砂丘 keyword. Returns Tottori Dunes (expected, query asked 'not just Tottori' so Tottori presence is acceptable), plus Onjuku Beach (Chiba), Ishikari Museum (Hokkaido sand dune museum), Fukiage Beach (Kagoshima). Good national distribution. Minor: municipal scrape noise (Tottori film commission page) included.
missing_data: Sarobetsu sand dune (Hokkaido), Nakatajima (Shizuoka), Kotobikihama (Kyoto); some entries lack coordinates or English descriptions.
improvement_hint: Filter out municipal promotional pages (film commission etc.) from sand dune keyword results; add Nakatajima and Sarobetsu dunes to corpus.

reasoning: Returns 4 Shimane METI craft designations with full Chinese translations: Iwami Yaki ceramics, Sekishu Washi paper, Unshuu Soroban abacus, Izumo stone lanterns. missing_data: Tottori crafts (Inshuu Washi, Tottori Mingei) excluded since tool called Shimane only; see_also_wikidata_venues points to Iwami Ginzan Mine (appropriate).
improvement_hint: Add region_fanout for San'in (Shimane+Tottori) when 山陰 region keyword detected; currently only single prefecture returned.

reasoning: Query asks for Hokkaido lavender fields beyond Furano. Response returns generic Hokkaido highlights: Showa-shinzan volcano, Sapporo Clock Tower, Matsumae Castle—no lavender fields at all. Lavender kind/tag not surfaced, no Furano alternative locations found.

reasoning: Query asks for traditional towns (古い町並み/tradisyonal na bayan) in Kyushu but in Tagalog 'tradisyonal na bayan ng kape' means traditional coffee towns—possible misread. Taking intent as traditional townscapes: returns Oita castles and shrines (Usuki Castle, Usa Jingu, Oka Castle) which are not preserved townscape districts. Even for 'historic towns' the result misses preservation districts.

reasoning: Query asks for kominka (traditional farmhouse stays) in Hokuriku region. Returns Ishikawa hotels: two hito_yu_kai onsen ryokans (Shirayama area), 'Student housing 1' (OSM noise), AB Hotel (business hotel). No kominka at all returned. Both onsen ryokan and business hotel miss the specific kominka-stay constraint.
missing_data: Kominka stay accommodations in Ishikawa/Toyama/Fukui; Gokayama/Shirakawa-go (UNESCO WHS gassho-zukuri farmhouses) are in neighboring Toyama/Gifu—highly relevant. Nanto City gassho-style stays.

reasoning: Excellent result. Returns Kongōbu-ji (Koyasan head temple), Negoro-ji, Kumano Kodō (UNESCO pilgrimage routes), Kumano Sanzan (three grand shrines)—all core Kii Peninsula mountain pilgrimage sites. Tool scope note correctly flags UNESCO vs Japan Heritage distinction. Well-structured with heritage designations and coordinates.
missing_data: Yoshino-Omine area (Nara side of Kii); Kumano Hongu Taisha individual entry; Ise Jingu (Mie, adjacent peninsula) not in Wakayama scope but relevant for mountain faith context.
improvement_hint: Cross-reference Nara/Mie prefectures for Kii Peninsula mountain shrine queries since the pilgrimage network spans three prefectures.

reasoning: Query asks for summer fireworks festivals along the Sea of Japan coast. Returns 129 Niigata events but top items are cherry blossom festival (May), lily festival—not fireworks. Nagaoka Grand Fireworks (one of Japan's top 3, August) appears third after unrelated spring events. Only Niigata covered; Akita, Toyama, Ishikawa Sea of Japan coast excluded.
missing_data: Omagari Fireworks (Akita, Sea of Japan coast), Nagaoka ranked higher; Toyama/Ishikawa fireworks; hanabi/summer filter not applied to surface most relevant items first.

reasoning: Returns Beppu Bamboo Crafts (別府竹細工) as second item with full description—exactly what was asked. First item is Hakata Ori textile (weaving, Fukuoka/Oita joint production area—technically accurate since Oita is in production area). Well-described with production areas and METI designation. see_also venues point to history museum.
missing_data: Beppu Bamboo Craft Museum venue data and workshop experience info; Oita Bamboo Craft Center address.

reasoning: Query asks for cycling routes through rural Shikoku. Returns Ehime spots—castles (Ozu, Yuzuki, Uwajima) and Ōmishima Island (cycling_route kind noted). Ōmishima is Shimanami Kaidō relevant and directly useful. However rural cycling route intent is not well served by castle-heavy list; Kochi/Tokushima/Kagawa cycling options absent.

reasoning: Query asks where to see kabuki outside Tokyo/Kyoto. Returns 183 traditional arts items but top results are Bunraku, then Kabuki (UNESCO entry—no venue/location), then Chakkirako dance, Dainichido Bugaku. The kabuki entry has no venue data, no location outside Tokyo/Kyoto. Regional kabuki venues (Kanamaruza in Kagawa, Eitaro-za) absent.
